creator: mij@sshguard.net
text:
(itetcu@ and leeym@ cc-ed FTK)


> sysutils/sshguard (and sshguard-ipfw/sshguard-pf) when installed
> out-of-the-box relies upon the syslog.conf "|" mechanism to run
> sshguard. There's one major issue with this method, as I found
> out this morning: newsyslog sends a SIGHUP to syslogd at midnight
> (so it will reopen its logfiles), thus the pipe to sshguard gets
> closed and sshguard is respawned.
>
> When sshguard is respawned, it empties the pf <sshguard> table
> of all entries. Therefore every night at midnight, you lose all
> blocked IPs which sshguard has spent time adding over the past day.

sshguard works differently to other DoS protecting systems here: it does
not simply throw addresses to block into the firewall, but it  
withdraws them
after a time interval chosen randomly between two bounds.
See the "-p" option in
http://sshguard.sourceforge.net/doc/sshguard8-1x.html

Flushing addresses on exit guarantees consistency wrt this policy,  
and letting
sshguard live forever does not help keeping addresses.

Briefly, a manifold of reasons is behind this policy:
- legitimate users could be got blocked and the blocking system should
not deny service forever to them
- real attackers do not typically insist on a host once they are  
blocked. The
default deny time does indeed maximize the chance to discourage this for
excessive ineffefficiency: 4 attempts cost 15 mins shortage
- a lot of ISPs provide dynamic IP addresses. It is useless (real  
attacker
not blocked after IP change) and unsafe (legitimate user could take a  
blocked
address over) to block such addresses forever


> To alleviate this, I decided it would be best to make a startup
> rc.d script for sshguard which did tail -n0 -F /var/log/auth.log |  
> sshguard &.
>
> It would be benefitial if someone could review the rc.d script
> for mistakes/oversights. It does work (tested on RELENG_6), but
> I did not try all of the rc.subr commands (status/poll/etc.).
> Just stop/start/restart. I'm particularly concerned over
> which rcorder(8) strings are needed.
>
> Also: the port installation mechanism would need to be changed
> to not modify syslog.conf, because that mechanism won't be
> needed any longer. :-)

please assess the discussion for these PRs
ports/112749
ports/113453
